node-anatomy: CONTENT: Anatomy of ONE Ray node. A single big dashed-teal rounded container labeled "Node". Inside, top: a cream-beige box "Raylet" containing two sub-boxes: "Scheduler" (cream) and a teal box "Object Store (Plasma)". Below: a vivid purple box "Worker", and inside/attached to it two small boxes: "Ownership Table" (light lavender) and "In-process Store" (light lavender). To the side, a magenta-pink box "GCS (head only)" and an olive-gold box "Driver". A short dark-red caption points at Raylet: "one Raylet per node: schedules + stores big objects".
memory: CONTENT: Four kinds of memory in Ray, shown as four labeled rounded boxes in a row, each a different flat color: 1) purple "Task/Actor Heap" (running user code), 2) teal "Shared Object Store" (big objects, cross-node), 3) light lavender "In-process Store" (small objects < 100KB), 4) cream-beige "System Metadata" (~KB per ObjectRef). Under each box a one-line English caption of what it holds. A dark-red caption at the bottom: "big objects -> shared store; small objects -> in-process".
cluster: CONTENT: Ray cluster overview. Three dashed-teal rounded Node containers side by side labeled "Node 1", "Node 2", "Node 3", each containing a cream "Scheduler", a purple "Worker", and a teal "Object Store". One of them (Node 1, the head node) additionally has a magenta-pink box "GCS" and an olive-gold "Driver" on top. Thin black double-arrows connect the three Schedulers (distributed scheduling) and connect the three Object Stores (object transfer). A dark-red caption points at GCS: "GCS lives only on the head node".
concepts: CONTENT: The five core Ray concepts as five labeled rounded boxes with a one-line English definition under each: purple "Task" (a remote function call, returns an ObjectRef), teal "Object" (immutable value, referenced by ObjectRef), purple "Actor" (stateful worker), olive-gold "Driver" (program entry, submits tasks), light lavender "Job" (all tasks/objects of one Driver). Arrange in a clean row or 2x3 grid. A dark-red caption: ".remote() returns an ObjectRef immediately, not the result".
